metadata.text: Remarkable progress has been made in the development of new therapies for cancer, dramatically changing the landscape of treatment approaches for several malignancies and continuing to increase patient survival.

metadata.title: Small molecules in targeted cancer therapy: advances, challenges, and future perspectives
metadata.text: Due to the advantages in efficacy and safety compared with traditional chemotherapy drugs, targeted therapeutic drugs have become mainstream cancer treatments.

metadata.title: Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer in 2022: A Review for General Practitioners in Oncology.
metadata.text: Here, we present a contemporary review of treatment of non-small cell lung cancer in Canada based on current best practices.

metadata.text: Cisplatin-based chemotherapy was the frequently used therapeutic option.

metadata.title: Cancer Stem Cells in Prostate Cancer: Implications for Targeted Therapy.
metadata.text: Therapies targeting CSCs might therefore lead to more effective cancer treatments, divergent from a traditional anti-proliferative approach, based on tumor bulk reduction accompanied by CSC-specific inhibition.

metadata.title: Cisplatin and Radiation Therapy With or Without Carboplatin and Paclitaxel in Patients With Locally Advanced Cervical Cancer
metadata.text: This randomized phase III trial studies how well giving cisplatin and radiation therapy together with or without carboplatin and paclitaxel works in treating patients with cervical cancer has spread from where it started to nearby tissue or lymph nodes.

metadata.title: Radiation treatment of lung cancer--patterns of practice in Canada.
metadata.study_type: Comparative Study, Journal Article, Research Support, Non-U.S. Gov't
metadata.text: Canadian practice in the treatment of lung cancers shows some variations although it is consistent with the trends in the literature.

metadata.title: Immunization With IMM-101 vs Observation for Prevention of Respiratory and Severe COVID-19 Related Infections in Cancer Patients at Increased Risk of Exposure
metadata.text: It has been studied in over 300 cancer patients who have also been receiving other cancer treatments, including chemotherapy and radiation, and seems promising, but it is not clear if it can offer better results than not having the immunization at all.

metadata.title: Systemic and Topical Treatments for Rash Secondary to Erlotinib in Lung Cancer
metadata.text: In July 2005, erlotinib was approved in Canada for the treatment of patients with locally advanced or metastatic NSCLC, following failure of first or second-line chemotherapy.

metadata.title: S0802 - Topotecan With or Without Aflibercept in Treating Patients With Extensive-Stage Small Cell Lung Cancer
metadata.text: This randomized phase II trial is studying topotecan to see how well it works when given with or without aflibercept in treating patients with extensive-stage small cell lung cancer.

metadata.title: Gemcitabine Hydrochloride and Cisplatin With or Without Bevacizumab in Treating Patients With Advanced Urinary Tract Cancer
metadata.text: This randomized phase III trial studies gemcitabine hydrochloride, cisplatin, and bevacizumab to see how well they work compared with gemcitabine hydrochloride and cisplatin in treating patients with urinary tract cancer that has spread to other places in the body.

metadata.title: Shooting for the Moon: Can We Cut Cancer Mortality in Canada By 50% By 2050?
metadata.text: In this study, we estimated how and whether a similar cancer control initiative could be achieved in Canada.

metadata.title: Leptomeningeal Metastases.
metadata.text: Hopefully more formal studies will be conducted in the future to verify which treatments are indeed most effective for particular types of cancer.

metadata.title: Accelerated Checkpoint Therapy for Any Steroid Dependent Patient With Brain Metastases
metadata.text: Treatment with immunotherapy has been shown to be effective in a wide range of cancers, including melanoma skin cancer, lung cancer and kidney cancer, among others.

metadata.title: S0221 Adjuvant Doxorubicin, Cyclophosphamide, and Paclitaxel in Treating Patients With Breast Cancer
metadata.text: It is not yet known which combination chemotherapy regimen is more effective in treating resected breast cancer.

metadata.title: Small molecules in targeted cancer therapy: advances, challenges, and future perspectives
metadata.text: We present all the approved drugs as well as important drug candidates in clinical trials for each target, discuss the current challenges, and provide insights and perspectives for the research and development of anti-cancer drugs.

metadata.title: S0802 - Topotecan With or Without Aflibercept in Treating Patients With Extensive-Stage Small Cell Lung Cancer
metadata.text: Evaluate the efficacy of topotecan hydrochloride with vs without aflibercept (ziv-aflibercept), in terms of progression-free survival at 3 months, in patients with extensive stage small cell lung cancer previously treated with platinum-based therapy.

metadata.title: Lung Cancer.
metadata.text: Treatment is determined by subtype and stage of cancer; there are several personalized therapies that did not exist just a few years ago.

metadata.title: Mistletoe as Complementary Treatment in Patients With Advanced Non-small-cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC), Treated With Carboplatin/Gemcitabine Chemotherapy Combination.
metadata.text: Other clinical data, although not well based is that complementary treatment when used in combination with the common oncology treatment has tumor response effect.

metadata.title: Combination Chemotherapy and Tamoxifen in Treating Patients With Solid Tumors
metadata.text: OBJECTIVES: * Determine the feasibility and efficacy of cisplatin, doxorubicin, and tamoxifen (CAT) in patients with soft tissue sarcoma, glioma, mesothelioma, hepatoma, thyroid cancer, or adrenal cancer.

metadata.title: Vaccine Therapy Plus Radiation Therapy in Treating Patients With Non-small Cell Lung Cancer That Has Been Completely Removed in Surgery
metadata.text: Combining these two treatments may kill more tumor cells.

metadata.title: Combination Chemotherapy With or Without Ganitumab in Treating Patients With Newly Diagnosed Metastatic Ewing Sarcoma
metadata.text: This randomized phase III trial studies how well combination chemotherapy with or without ganitumab works in treating patients with newly diagnosed Ewing sarcoma that has spread to other parts of the body.

metadata.title: The FDA approval of pembrolizumab for patients with TMB >10 mut/Mb: was it a wise decision? No
metadata.text: Specifically, the three most common cancers—small cell lung cancer [34 patients (pts)], cervical cancer (16 pts), and endometrial cancer (15 pts) account for 64% of 102 patients for which approval is based, but all three cancers already have approvals for pembrolizumab as a single agent or in combination.10Center for Drug Evaluation and ResearchFDA approves pembrolizumab for advanced cervical cancer with disease progression during or after chemotherapy.

metadata.title: Niraparib Efficacy in Patient With Unresectable Mesothelioma
metadata.text: This cancer is incurable and lacks effective treatment, particularly after initial chemotherapy.

metadata.title: Challenges in Management of Squamous Cell Carcinoma of the Anus in New England and Across the United States: A Review of the National Cancer Data Base.
metadata.text: A review of available data from the American College of Surgeons Committee on Cancer National Cancer Data Base focused on incidence, sex, age, stage at diagnosis, distance traveled for care, and utilization of therapy as first course of treatment (FCT).

metadata.title: Testing the Addition of an Antibody to Standard Chemoradiation Followed by the Antibody for One Year to Standard Chemoradiation Followed by One Year of the Antibody in Patients With Unresectable Stage III Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
metadata.text: This phase III trial studies how well an antibody (durvalumab) with chemotherapy and radiation therapy (chemoradiation) works in treating patients with stage III non-small cell lung cancer that cannot be removed by surgery (unresectable).

metadata.title: Access to Cancer Drugs in Canada.
metadata.text: Lung cancer, because of the multiple subtypes now identifiable and because of the myriad of new and effective therapies, provides fertile ground to highlight issues related to oncology drug access in Canada [...].

metadata.title: S0802 - Topotecan With or Without Aflibercept in Treating Patients With Extensive-Stage Small Cell Lung Cancer
metadata.text: It is not yet known whether topotecan is more effective with or without aflibercept in treating patients with small cell lung cancer.

metadata.title: Progress in site-specific cancer mortality in Canada over the last 70 years.
metadata.text: There has been a significant reduction in cancer mortality in Canada since site-specific cancer mortality rates peaked decades ago for many cancers.

metadata.title: A Window of Opportunity Trial of Afatinib In Early Stage Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (NSCLC)
metadata.text: There are several other cancer drugs that bind to these receptors to slow or stop cancer growth such as Gefitinib and erlotinib which are FDA approved and used for the treatment of NSCLC.

metadata.title: Combination Chemotherapy in Treating Young Patients With Newly Diagnosed Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia
metadata.text: Giving more than one drug (combination chemotherapy) may kill more cancer cells.

metadata.title: Radiation Therapy With Durvalumab or Cetuximab in Treating Patients With Locoregionally Advanced Head and Neck Cancer Who Cannot Take Cisplatin
metadata.text: This phase II/III trial studies how well radiation therapy works with durvalumab or cetuximab in treating patients with head and neck cancer that has spread to a local and/or regional area of the body who cannot take cisplatin.

metadata.title: Demystifying the Role of Immuno PET-CT in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer: Clinical Value and Research Trends.
metadata.text: The management of Lung cancer, especially non-small cell lung cancer has undergone a paradigm shift recently with the advent of new treatment approaches including focused radiotherapy as well as evolution of a newer class of immunotherapy agents.
